prompt_text: Just tell me the single best value cordless drill to buy right now for home DIY. Give me one answer.
response_text:
## DIY Home Drill Recommendation: DeWalt DCD777C2

**DeWalt DCD777C2 20V MAX Cordless Drill/Driver Kit** — around **$99-129**

**Why this one:**
- **Brushless motor** at this price point is unusual — means longer runtime, more power, and better durability than brushed competitors in the same range
- Comes as a **complete kit**: drill, 2 batteries, charger, and case — no surprise extra costs
- **DeWalt's 20V MAX battery system** is huge — if you ever buy other DeWalt tools (saw, impact driver, flashlight), batteries are interchangeable
- Lightweight and compact enough for everyday tasks (hanging shelves, assembling furniture, small projects) but has enough torque for occasional heavier jobs (decking, larger screws)
- Two-speed gearbox handles both drilling and driving well

**The honest caveat:** If you truly only drill a few holes a year, a $40 basic drill will "work." But for anyone doing regular home DIY — shelving, furniture, small repairs — the DCD777C2 hits the sweet spot of price, reliability, and a battery platform you can grow into.

**Where to get it:** Home Depot often has this exact kit on rollback pricing; also check Amazon and Lowe's for comparable deals.

If you want, tell me your specific use case (heavy-duty projects vs. occasional light use) and I can fine-tune the pick.
